kjv_text: And I bowed down my head, and worshipped the LORD, and blessed the LORD God of my master Abraham, which had led me in the right way to take my master's brother's daughter unto his son.
issue: Theological term אֱמֶת (emet) = truth, faithfulness, reliability not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ona fai atu ai lea o Iakopo i lona tamā, O a'u o Esau o lau ulumatua, ua ou faia le mea na e fai mai ai ia te au; ina nofo ia i luga, ia nofo ma 'ai i la'u manu ina ia e faamanuia mai ia te au.
samoan_glosses: Ona fai atu ai lea=and said to then then; o Iakopo=Jacob; i lona tamā,=in his father; O a'u=I am; o Esau=Esau; o lau ulumatua,=your the first-born; ua ou faia=I made; le mea=the thing
kjv_text: And Jacob said unto his father, I am Esau thy firstborn; I have done according as thou badest me: arise, I pray thee, sit and eat of my venison, that thy soul may bless me.
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Na faia foi e ia le mea e 'ai e manogi, ma avatu i lona tamā, ua faapea atu i lona tamā, Lo'u tamā e, ia e nofo i luga, ma 'ai i le manu a lou atalii, ina ia e faamanuia mai ia te au.
samoan_glosses: Na faia foi=made also; e ia le mea=by him the thing; e 'ai=to eat; e manogi,=to fragrant; ma avatu=and give away; i lona tamā,=in his father; ua faapea atu=and said; i lona tamā,=in his father
kjv_text: And he also had made savoury meat, and brought it unto his father, and said unto his father, Let my father arise, and eat of his son's venison, that thy soul may bless me.
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Na faia e Ieova le Atua i le eleele o manu vaefa uma lava, ma manu felelei uma lava; na ia aumaia ia Atamu ia iloa pe ni a ni igoa na te faaigoa ai; o le igoa ua faaigoaina ai e Atamu i meaola uma o l
samoan_glosses: Na faia=And formed; e Ieova=the LORD; le Atua=God; i le eleele=the ground; o manu vaefa uma lava,=beasts all indeed; ma manu felelei uma lava;=and every bird of the air; na ia aumaia ia=and brought them; Atamu ia iloa=to Adam to see
kjv_text: And out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought them unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creatu
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: O lenei, sau ia, ina fai ia sa ta mavaega; ia fai foi lea ma molimau ia te i taua.
samoan_glosses: O lenei,=now; sau ia,=come to; ina fai ia=so that make to; sa ta mavaega;=a stroke a parting command; ia fai foi=to make also; lea=lea; ma molimau ia te=and a witness unto; i taua.=in precious
kjv_text: Now therefore come thou, let us make a covenant, I and thou; and let it be for a witness between me and thee.
issue: Theological term בְּרִית (berit) = binding covenant agreement not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ona fasi lea e Iakopo ni manu i le mauga, ua fai atu foi i ona uso e o mai e aai; ona latou aai ai lea, ua latou nonofo pea foi i le mauga i lea po.
samoan_glosses: Ona fasi lea=and kill; e Iakopo ni manu=to Jacob some animal; i le mauga,=in the mountain; ua fai atu foi=said to also; i ona uso=in then brother; e o mai=by of from; e aai;=to city; ona latou aai ai lea,=and they city then then
kjv_text: Then Jacob offered sacrifice upon the mount, and called his brethren to eat bread: and they did eat bread, and tarried all night in the mount.
issue: Theological term זֶבַח (zevach) = sacrifice, slaughter not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ou te aioi atu, ia e laveai mai ia te au ai le lima o lo'u uso, o le lima lava o Esau; auā ou te fefe ia te ia, ina ne'i sau ia ma fasia a'u, ma tinā, atoa ma fanau.
samoan_glosses: Ou te aioi atu,=I will cry out; ia e laveai=to to deliver; mai ia te au=from me; ai=ai; le lima=the five; o lo'u uso,=my brother; o le lima=the five; lava o Esau;=indeed of Esau
kjv_text: Deliver me, I pray thee, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau: for I fear him, lest he will come and smite me, and the mother with the children.
issue: Theological term בְּרִית (berit) = binding covenant agreement not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ua tautala mai foi Hamoro ia te i latou, ua faapea mai, O lo'u atalii o Sekema, o loo ua manao tele lava o ia i lo outou teine; se'i outou tuu mai ia o ia ia te ia e fai mana avā.
samoan_glosses: Ua tautala mai foi=spoke from also; Hamoro ia te=Hamoro unto; i latou,=them; ua faapea mai,=and said; O lo'u atalii=my son; o Sekema,=Shechem; o loo=is/are (progressive); ua manao tele lava=desired great indeed
kjv_text: And Hamor communed with them, saying, The soul of my son Shechem longeth for your daughter: I pray you give her him to wife.
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Sa ia Iosefa Ieova, ua ia alofa ia te ia, ua na faatupuina le alofa o le alii o le fale puipui ia te ia.
samoan_glosses: Sa ia Iosefa=to Joseph; Ieova,=the LORD; ua ia alofa ia te ia,=to loved to him; ua=ua; na faatupuina=raised up; le alofa=the love; o le alii=the Lord; o le fale puipui=the house a duor
kjv_text: But the LORD was with Joseph, and shewed him mercy, and gave him favour in the sight of the keeper of the prison.
issue: Theological term בְּרִית (berit) = binding covenant agreement not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ua faalogo i laua i le siufofoga o Ieova le Atua o loo savali i le faatoaga i le itu aso e agi malie mai ai le matagi; ona lalafi ai lea o Atamu ma lana avā ai luma o Ieova le Atua i laau o le faatoag
samoan_glosses: Ua faalogo=Then heard; i laua=they; i le siufofoga=the voice; o Ieova le Atua=of the LORD God; o loo savali=walking; i le faatoaga=in the garden; i le itu aso=in the cool part/time of the day; e agi malie mai ai le matagi;=when the wind blows softly
kjv_text: And they heard the voice of the LORD God walking in the garden in the cool of the day: and Adam and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the LORD God amongst the trees of the garden.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: A o lei mavae aso e tolu ona faaea lea e Farao i lou ulu, ma toe tofia oe i lou faiva, e te tuu atu ai foi le ipu a Farao i lona aao, faapei o anamua ina o e tautu uaina ia te ia.
samoan_glosses: A o lei mavae aso=but of not passed day; e tolu=by three; ona faaea lea=and faaea; e Farao=to Pharaoh; i lou ulu,=in your head; ma toe tofia oe=and again chosen you; i lou faiva,=in your ministry; e te tuu atu ai=by placed
kjv_text: Yet within three days shall Pharaoh lift up thine head, and restore thee unto thy place: and thou shalt deliver Pharaoh's cup into his hand, after the former manner when thou wast his butler.
issue: Theological term מִשְׁפָּט (mishpat) = justice, judgment, ordinance not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: A ia e manatua mai a'u pe a oo le manuia ia te oe, ma ia e alofa mai ia te au, ma ia e ta'ua a'u ia Farao, ma ia aveina atu a'u i lenei fale;
samoan_glosses: A ia=but to; e manatua mai a'u=to remember from me; pe a oo=even though / although oo; le manuia ia te oe,=the peace unto you; ma ia=and to; e alofa mai ia te au,=to love from me; ma ia=and to; e ta'ua a'u=by us (two, incl) me
kjv_text: But think on me when it shall be well with thee, and shew kindness, I pray thee, unto me, and make mention of me unto Pharaoh, and bring me out of this house:
issue: Theological term בְּרִית (berit) = binding covenant agreement not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ona tali atu ai lea o Reupena ia te i latou, ua faapea atu, Ou te lei fai atu ea ia te outou, faapea, Aua ne'i outou agaleaga atu i le tama, a ua outou le faalogo mai lava? Faauta foi, ua tauia mai lo
samoan_glosses: Ona tali atu ai lea=and respond then then; o Reupena ia te=Reuben unto; i latou,=them; ua faapea atu,=and said; Ou te lei fai atu ea=I will not said to indeed?/really?; ia te outou,=unto you; faapea,=thus; Aua ne'i outou=for now you (all)
kjv_text: And Reuben answered them, saying, Spake I not unto you, saying, Do not sin against the child; and ye would not hear? therefore, behold, also his blood is required.

samoan_text: Ona ave ai lea e le nuu ia mea alofa, ma tupe ave lua i o latou lima, atoa foi ma Peniamina; ona latou tulai ai lea, ma o ifo i Aikupito, ma latou tutū i luma o Iosefa.
samoan_glosses: Ona ave ai lea=and take then then; e le nuu=shall not nation; ia mea alofa,=to thing love; ma tupe ave lua=and St. Thomas bean take two; i o latou=in of they; lima,=five; atoa foi=together with also; ma Peniamina;=and Benjamin
kjv_text: And the men took that present, and they took double money in their hand, and Benjamin; and rose up, and went down to Egypt, and stood before Joseph.

samoan_text: Ona latou sauni lea le mea alofa, a o lei sau Iosefa i le aoauli, auā na latou faalogo o le a latou aai i lea mea.

kjv_text: And they made ready the present against Joseph came at noon: for they heard that they should eat bread there.
issue: Theological term קָרְבָּן (qorban) = offering, that which is brought near not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ua sau Iosefa i le fale, ona latou au mai ai lea ia te ia i le fale le mea alofa nai o latou lima, ma latou ifo toele mai ia te ia.
samoan_glosses: Ua sau Iosefa=came Joseph; i le fale,=in the house; ona latou au mai ai lea=and they bring then then; ia te ia=to him; i le fale=in the house; le mea alofa=the thing love; nai o latou=from of they; lima,=five

issue: Theological term קָרְבָּן (qorban) = offering, that which is brought near not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E fai a'u mona moni; e te saili mai o ia ia te au; afai ou te le toe au mai o ia ia te oe, ma tuu ia te ia i ou luma, ona ou nofo sala ai lea ia te oe i aso uma lava.
samoan_glosses: E fai a'u=to make me; mona moni;=to burst forth (as faiai true; e te saili=by seek; mai o ia ia te au;=from he unto me; afai ou te=if I will; le toe au mai=the again bring; o ia ia te oe,=he unto you; ma tuu ia te ia=and put to him
kjv_text: I will be surety for him; of my hand shalt thou require him: if I bring him not unto thee, and set him before thee, then let me bear the blame for ever:
issue: Theological term חַטָּאת (chattat) = sin, sin offering not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: O lenei, pe a a'u alu atu i lau auauna o lo'u tamā, a e le o ia te i matou le tama, (auā o le ola o le tama e ola ai o ia,)
samoan_glosses: O lenei,=now; pe a a'u=even though / although me; alu atu=go away; i lau auauna=in your servant; o lo'u tamā,=my father; a=but; e le o ia te=by the he; i matou=in we (excl)
kjv_text: Now therefore when I come to thy servant my father, and the lad be not with us; seeing that his life is bound up in the lad's life;

samoan_text: Auā na fai lau auauna ma moni o le tama i lo'u tamā, ua faapea atu, Afai ou te le toe aumaia o ia ia te oe, ona ou nofo sala ai lea i lo'u tamā i aso uma.
samoan_glosses: Auā=for; na fai=made; lau auauna=your servant; ma moni=and true; o le tama=the child; i lo'u tamā,=in my father; ua faapea atu,=and said; Afai ou te=if I will
kjv_text: For thy servant became surety for the lad unto my father, saying, If I bring him not unto thee, then I shall bear the blame to my father for ever.
issue: Theological term חַטָּאת (chattat) = sin, sin offering not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: O tama tane lava i latou a Lea, na ia fanau ia Iakopo i Patanarama, atoa ma lona afafine o Tina; o ona atalii atoa ia ma ona afafine ua toatolugafulu ma le toatolu.

issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: O tagata uma na o mai ma Iakopo i Aikupito, e na tutupu mai ia te ia, a e le faitaulia avā a atalii o Iakopo, o ona atoa o tagata uma e toaonogafulu ma le toaono.

issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: O atalii o Iosefa e na fanau ia te ia i Aikupito, o tama e toalua; o tagata atoa uma o le aiga o Iakopo e na o mai i Aikupito e toafitugafulu lava i latou.

samoan_text: O atalii o Iosefa e na fanau ia te ia i Aikupito, o tama e toalua; o tagata atoa uma o le aiga o Iakopo e na o mai i Aikupito e toafitugafulu lava i latou.
samoan_glosses: O atalii o Iosefa=sons Joseph; e=by; na fanau ia te ia=born to him; i Aikupito,=in Egypt; o tama e=child by; toalua;=two (people); o tagata=of men; atoa uma=together with all
kjv_text: And the sons of Joseph, which were born him in Egypt, were two souls: all the souls of the house of Jacob, which came into Egypt, were threescore and ten.

samoan_text: Ua manatua e le Atua Noa, ma mea ola uma, ma manu vaefa uma sa i le vaa ma ia. Ua faaagi atu e le Atua le matagi i le lalolagi; ona maui ai lea o le vai;
samoan_glosses: Ua manatua=remembered; e le Atua=God; Noa,=Noah; ma mea ola uma,=and every living thing; ma manu vaefa uma=and all the animals; sa i le vaa ma ia.=that were with him in the ark; Ua faaagi atu=then caused to pass; e le Atua=God
kjv_text: And God remembered Noah, and every living thing, and all the cattle that was with him in the ark: and God made a wind to pass over the earth, and the waters asswaged;
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: atoa foi ma mea ola uma o ia te outou, o manu felelei, o manu vaefa fanua, ma manu o le vao uma o ia te outou, o mea uma ua ulufafo i le vaa e oo i manu uma o le lalolagi.
samoan_glosses: atoa foi ma mea ola uma=and with every living creature; o ia te outou,=that is with you; o manu felelei,=birds; o manu vaefa fanua,=cattle; ma manu o le vao uma=and every beast of the earth; o ia te outou,=with you; o mea uma ua ulufafo i le vaa=all that go out of the ark; e oo i manu uma o le lalolagi.=to every beast of the earth
kjv_text: And with every living creature that is with you, of the fowl, of the cattle, and of every beast of the earth with you; from all that go out of the ark, to every beast of the earth.

samoan_text: Ua fetalai atu foi le Atua, O le faailoga lenei o le feagaiga ou te faia ma outou, atoa ma mea ola uma o ia te outou, e autupulaga lava;
samoan_glosses: Ua fetalai atu foi=said; le Atua,=God; O le faailoga lenei=this is the sign; o le feagaiga=of the covenant; ou te faia=I make; ma outou,=with you; atoa ma mea ola uma o ia te outou,=and every living creature with you; e autupulaga lava;=for perpetual generations
kjv_text: And God said, This is the token of the covenant which I make between me and you and every living creature that is with you, for perpetual generations:

samoan_text: ona ou manatua lea o la'u feagaiga ua ou osia ma outou, ma mea ola eseese uma lava; e le toe avea le vai ma lolo e faaumatia ai mea ola uma.
samoan_glosses: ona ou manatua lea=I will remember; o la'u feagaiga=my covenant; ua ou osia ma outou,=which is between Me and you; ma mea ola eseese uma lava;=and every living creature of all flesh; e le toe avea le vai=the waters shall never again become; ma lolo=a flood; e faaumatia ai mea ola uma.=to destroy all flesh
kjv_text: And I will remember my covenant, which is between me and you and every living creature of all flesh; and the waters shall no more become a flood to destroy all flesh.

samoan_text: E i ai foi le nuanua i le ao, ou te vaai foi i ai, e manatua ai le feagaiga e faavavau ua osia e a'u o le Atua ma mea ola eseese uma lava o i le lalolagi.
samoan_glosses: E i ai foi=shall be; le nuanua=the rainbow; i le ao,=in the cloud; ou te vaai foi i ai,=and I will look upon it; e manatua ai=to remember; le feagaiga e faavavau=the everlasting covenant; ua osia e a'u o le Atua=between Me God; ma mea ola eseese uma lava=and every living creature
kjv_text: And the bow shall be in the cloud; and I will look upon it, that I may remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is upon the earth.

samoan_text: A o le aano o manu o i ai le ola, o lona toto foi lea , aua tou te aai ai.
samoan_glosses: A o le aano o manu=but the flesh of animals; o i ai le ola,=with its life; o lona toto foi lea ,=that is its blood; aua tou te aai ai.=you shall not eat

issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E moni lava, ou te taui atu lo outou toto o lo outou ola; ou te tauiatua i manu uma; o le tagata foi, ou te taui atu le ola o le tagata i le tagata lona uso.
samoan_glosses: E moni lava,=surely; ou te taui atu=I will require; lo outou toto=your blood; o lo outou ola;=of your lives; ou te tauiatua=I will require; i manu uma;=at every beast; o le tagata foi,=and from man; ou te taui atu=I will require

kjv_text: It shall even be as when an hungry man dreameth, and, behold, he eateth; but he awaketh, and his soul is empty: or as when a thirsty man dreameth, and, behold, he drinketh; but he awaketh, and, behold
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E faamasino foi o ia i nuu ese, ma aoai atu i nuu e tele; ona latou tu'i ai lea i a latou pelu e fai ma uamea o suotosina, o latou tao foi e fai ma polo e teu a'i vine; e le sii atu le pelu e le tasi
samoan_glosses: E faamasino foi=to judge also; o ia=he; i nuu ese,=in nation away; ma aoai atu=and chastise; i nuu e tele;=in nation to great; ona latou tu'i ai lea=and they '2l high chief then then; i=in; a latou pelu=but they sword
kjv_text: And he shall judge among the nations, and shall rebuke many people: and they shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruninghooks: nation shall not lift up sword against nation,
issue: Theological term צֶדֶק (tsedeq) = righteousness, justice not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: A e ui lava i lea e faatalitali Ieova ina ia alofa fua mai ia te outou; o le mea lea e tulai ai o ia e alofa ia te outou; auā o Ieova, o le Atua e faamasino tonu ia; amuia i latou uma o e faatalitali
samoan_glosses: A=but; e ui lava=to although indeed; i lea=in; e faatalitali Ieova=to waited the LORD; ina ia alofa fua mai ia te outou;=so that love fruit from you (all); o le mea lea=therefore; e tulai ai=to arise up; o ia=he
kjv_text: And therefore will the LORD wait, that he may be gracious unto you, and therefore will he be exalted, that he may have mercy upon you: for the LORD is a God of judgment: blessed are all they that wait
issue: Theological term מִשְׁפָּט (mishpat) = justice, judgment, ordinance not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: O lona mānava foi e pei o se vaitafe ua oso; e oo i le ua, e lulu ai nuu ese i le lulu e fano ai; e i ai foi i le gutu o le nuu le faagutu e faasese ai.
samoan_glosses: O lona mānava foi=his mānava also; e pei o se vaitafe=like a river; ua oso;=food for a journey; e oo=it shall come to pass; i le=in the; ua,=ua,; e lulu ai=to barn owl; nuu ese=nation away
kjv_text: And his breath, as an overflowing stream, shall reach to the midst of the neck, to sift the nations with the sieve of vanity: and there shall be a bridle in the jaws of the people, causing them to err
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: O mea uma foi e ui ane ai le sasa ua tofia, e faatumauina ai e Ieova i o latou luga, e faia ia ma topa ma kitara; e sii atu e ia le taua ia te i latou, o taua e luluina ai.

samoan_text: Faauta, e pule le tupu ma le amiotonu, e pule foi alii ma le tonu.
samoan_glosses: Faauta,=behold; e pule=to rule; le tupu=the king; ma le amiotonu,=and the righteous; e pule foi=to rule also; alii=Lord; ma le tonu.=and the right

samoan_text: E fai foi le tagata e pei o se lafitaga i le matagi, ma se mea e malu ai i le afā; e pei o vaitafe i le nuu naumati, e pei o le paolo foi o le papa tele i le nuu ua le o i ai se vai.
samoan_glosses: E fai foi=to make also; le tagata=the man; e pei o se lafitaga=like a a hiding place; i le matagi,=in the wind; ma se mea=and a thing; e malu ai=to female tattoo; i le afā;=in the cyclone; e pei o vaitafe=like river
kjv_text: And a man shall be as an hiding place from the wind, and a covert from the tempest; as rivers of water in a dry place, as the shadow of a great rock in a weary land.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: O togafiti a le mataoge e leaga ia; ua taupulepule ia i togafiti e faaumatia ai i upu pepelo e ua tigaina; e faaumatia foi e ua matitiva a o tautala i latou i upu tonu.
samoan_glosses: O togafiti=heal; a le mataoge=but the mataoge; e leaga ia;=to evil to; ua taupulepule ia=conspire to; i togafiti=in heal; e faaumatia ai=to destroyed; i upu pepelo=in word false; e ua tigaina;=by afflicted

issue: Theological term מִשְׁפָּט (mishpat) = justice, judgment, ordinance not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E totō outou o mutia magumagu, tou te fananau mai o tagutugutu o saito; o lo outou mānava o le afi lava lea e 'aina ai outou.
samoan_glosses: E totō outou=by totō you (all); o mutia magumagu,=the general name for grass withered up; tou te fananau=second person plural fananau; mai o tagutugutu=from of tagutugutu; o saito;=wheat; o lo outou mānava=your mānava; o le afi=the fire; lava lea=indeed

samoan_text: Auā e onā la'u pelu i le lagi; faauta, e alu ifo ia i luga o Etoma, i luga lava o le nuu ua ou faafanoina, ina ia faasalaina.

samoan_glosses: O au auauna=I am servant; na e aauina ai=you sent; ua e upu=you word; leaga ai=evil; i le Alii,=in the Lord; ua e faapea ane,=saying; Ua a'u alu ae=me went up; ma le tele=and the great

samoan_text: O ona po ia na aauina ai e Merota-palata le atalii o Palata, le tupu o Papelonia, nisi e au mai tusi ma le mea alofa ia Esekia; auā na ia faalogo, na ma'i o ia, a ua malolo.
samoan_glosses: O=O; ona po ia=and night to; na aauina ai=sent; e Merota-palata=by Merota-palata; le atalii o Palata,=the son of Palata; le tupu o Papelonia,=the king of Babylon; nisi e au mai tusi=some to bring book; ma le mea alofa=and the thing love

samoan_text: E afio mai Ieova e faamasino i toeaina o lona nuu, atoa ma o latou alii sili; auā ua outou faaumatia le tovine, o mea na faoa i e matitiva ua i ai ia i ou outou fale.

samoan_text: o pulou, ma filifili uamea i vae, ma fusi, ma fagu e sogi ai, atoa ma mea e sao ai;

issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Auā ua tausuai Ierusalema, ua paū foi Iuta; auā o latou laulaufaiva ma a latou amio, ua oo ia ia Ieova e faaonoono ai i luma o lana afio.

samoan_glosses: Ona faaalia mai ai lea=and revealed from then then; o le pupula=the brightness; o Ieova=the LORD; e vāaia lava=by vāaia indeed; e tagata uma faatasi;=to man all together; auā=for; ua fetalai mai ai=said; le fofoga o Ieova.=the face the LORD

issue: Theological term כָּבוֹד (kavod) = glory, weight, honor not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ua fai mai le leo, Ina alaga ia. Ona fai atu lea o ia, Se a ea se upu ou te alagaina? O tagata uma o le mutia i latou, ma lo latou lelei uma, e tusa lea ma le fuga o le vao;
samoan_glosses: Ua fai mai le=said the; leo,=voice; Ina alaga ia.=so that cry to; Ona fai atu lea=And he said; o ia,=he; Se a ea=what indeed? / why?; se upu ou te alagaina?=a word I will cry; O tagata uma=of men all

samoan_text: e magumagu le mutia, e toulu le fuga; auā o le mānava o Ieova ua agi mai i ai; e moni, o le mutia lava le nuu.

samoan_text: E te faasavili ia te i latou, e lelea foi i latou i le matagi; e faataapeapeina i latou i le afā; a o oe, e te olioli ia Ieova, e te mitamita foi i le Paia o Isaraelu.
samoan_glosses: E te faasavili=by make a breeze; ia te i latou,=unto them; e lelea foi=to to be carried away by the also; i latou=them; i le matagi;=in the wind; e faataapeapeina=to scattered; i latou=them; i le afā;=in the cyclone
kjv_text: Thou shalt fan them, and the wind shall carry them away, and the whirlwind shall scatter them: and thou shalt rejoice in the LORD, and shalt glory in the Holy One of Israel.

samoan_text: Faauta, o mea le aoga i latou uma; o a latou mea e fai ua matuā leai lava ia; o le matagi ma le mea faatauvaa a latou tupua uamea.
samoan_glosses: Faauta,=behold; o mea=thing; le aoga=the useful; i latou uma;=them all; o=o; a latou mea=but they thing; e fai=to make; ua matuā leai lava ia;=exceedingly no indeed to

samoan_text: o i latou uma ua igoa i lo'u igoa, na ou faia ia viia ai a'u; na a'u gaosia ma ou faia lava i latou.

samoan_text: Ua ou tauto ia te au lava, ua mavae atu le upu i lo'u gutu e faamaoni, e le toe foi mai lava, E tootuli mai ia te au o tulivae uma lava, e tauto mai laulaufaiva uma lava;

samoan_text: Ua tele foi le sala a le tama fafine a lo'u nuu i le sala a Sotoma le na faaumatia e pei o le emo o le mata, na le oo i ai ni lima.

kjv_text: For the punishment of the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the punishment of the sin of Sodom, that was overthrown as in a moment, and no hands stayed on her.

samoan_text: O le fouvale o le nuu e toatele ai ona alii; a o le tagata mafaufau ma le poto, e faaualoaina ai le malo .

samoan_text: O lē mulilua i le fafine ua leai sona mafaufau; o le na te faia lea mea, na te faaumatia e ia lona lava ola;

samoan_text: O le mata'u ia Ieova, o le 'ino'ino lea i le leaga; o le faamaualuga, ma le loto fefete, ma le amio leaga, ma le gutu faapiopio, ou te 'ino'ino i ai .

kjv_text: The fear of the LORD is to hate evil: pride, and arrogancy, and the evil way, and the froward mouth, do I hate.

samoan_text: Aua ne'i faaoo atu e se tasi le alofa ia te ia; aua foi ne'i agalelei atu se tasi i lana fanau ua aumatuā.

samoan_text: Ieova e, lo'u Atua, ia e fesoasoani mai ia te au; ia e faaola mai ia te au e tusa ma lou alofa;

samoan_text: Ia e faafofoga mai i lo'u leo e tusa ma lou alofa; Ieova e, ia e faaolaola mai ia te au e tusa ma lau faamasinoga.
samoan_glosses: Ia e faafofoga=to to listen; mai=from; i lo'u leo=in my voice; e tusa ma lou alofa;=according to your love; Ieova e,=O LORD!; ia e faaolaola=to to make the wedge in the handle; mai ia te au=from me; e tusa ma lau faamasinoga.=according to your judgment
kjv_text: Hear my voice according unto thy lovingkindness: O LORD, quicken me according to thy judgment.
issue: Theological term בְּרִית (berit) = binding covenant agreement not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ia e silasila mai ua ou naunau i au feau; Ieova e, ia e faaolaola mai ia te au e tusa ma lou alofa.
samoan_glosses: Ia e silasila mai=to to looked; ua ou naunau=I desired; i au feau;=in me errand; Ieova e,=O LORD!; ia e faaolaola=to to make the wedge in the handle; mai ia te au=from me; e tusa ma lou alofa.=according to your love
kjv_text: Consider how I love thy precepts: quicken me, O LORD, according to thy lovingkindness.
issue: Theological term בְּרִית (berit) = binding covenant agreement not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ia oo mai foi ia te au lou alofa, Ieova e, o lau olataga, e tusa ma lau afioga.
samoan_glosses: Ia oo mai foi=to came also; ia te au=unto me; lou alofa,=your love; Ieova e,=O LORD!; o lau olataga,=your salvation; e tusa ma lau afioga.=according to your word (divine)
kjv_text: Let thy mercies come also unto me, O LORD, even thy salvation, according to thy word.
issue: Theological term בְּרִית (berit) = binding covenant agreement not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ia e a'oa'o mai ia te au i le faautauta lelei ma le poto; auā ua ou faamaoni i au poloaiga.
samoan_glosses: Ia e a'oa'o=to to excellent; mai ia te au=from me; i le faautauta lelei=in the careful good; ma le poto;=and the wise; auā=for; ua ou faamaoni=I faithful; i au poloaiga.=in me commandment
kjv_text: Teach me good judgment and knowledge: for I have believed thy commandments.
issue: Theological term בָּטַח (batach) = trust, be confident, feel secure not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ia faasaga mai ia te au o e matatau ia te oe, atoa ma e ua iloa au tulafono.
samoan_glosses: Ia faasaga mai ia te au=to face from me; o=o; e matatau ia te oe,=to fear unto you; atoa ma=and; e ua iloa au=by knew me; tulafono.=law
kjv_text: Let those that fear thee turn unto me, and those that have known thy testimonies.
issue: Theological term שׁוּב (shuv) = turn, return, repent not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ia e faaolaola mai ia te au e tusa ma lou alofa, ona ou tausi lava lea i le tulafono a lou fofoga.
samoan_glosses: Ia e faaolaola=to to make the wedge in the handle; mai ia te au=from me; e tusa ma lou alofa,=according to your love; ona ou tausi=and I nurse; lava lea=indeed; i le tulafono=in the law; a lou fofoga.=but your face
kjv_text: Quicken me after thy lovingkindness; so shall I keep the testimony of thy mouth.
issue: Theological term בְּרִית (berit) = binding covenant agreement not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ou te faalagolago ia Ieova; se a le mea tou te fai mai ai ia te au, Ina sola ia e pei o le manulele i lo outou mauga?
samoan_glosses: Ou te faalagolago=I will rely; ia Ieova;=to the LORD; se a le mea tou te fai mai ai=what / why second person plural said; ia te au,=unto me; Ina sola ia=so that escape to; e pei o le manulele=like the bird; i lo=than; outou mauga?=you (all) mountain
kjv_text: In the LORD put I my trust: how say ye to my soul, Flee as a bird to your mountain?
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E tofotofo e Ieova i le ua amiotonu; a ua inosia e lona finagalo lē fai amio leaga, ma lē fiafia i le faasaua.
samoan_glosses: E tofotofo=to to try; e Ieova=the LORD; i le=in the; ua amiotonu;=righteous; a=but; ua inosia=abominable; e lona finagalo lē fai amio leaga,=by his a chiefs will or desire not make iniquity; ma lē fiafia=and not competition
kjv_text: The LORD trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loveth violence his soul hateth.
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E faatotō faaua mai e ia i luga o e fai amio leaga o mailei, o le afi ma le teio, o le matagi foi e manunu ai; o le tofi lea o la latou ipu.
samoan_glosses: E faatotō faaua=by faatotō faaua; mai e ia=from by him; i luga o=upon; e fai amio leaga=to make iniquity; o mailei,=a trap; o le afi=the fire; ma le teio,=and the brimstone; o le matagi foi=the wind also
kjv_text: Upon the wicked he shall rain snares, fire and brimstone, and an horrible tempest: this shall be the portion of their cup.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Auā ua tu ai nofoālii e faamasino ai, o nofoālii o le aiga o Tavita.
samoan_glosses: Auā=for; ua tu ai=stood; nofoālii=throne; e faamasino ai,=to judge; o nofoālii=throne; o le aiga=the family; o Tavita.=David

issue: Theological term מִשְׁפָּט (mishpat) = justice, judgment, ordinance not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Na tauto Ieova ia Tavita i le faamaoni, e le 'alo ese ai o ia, Ou te faanofoina i lou nofoālii so le fanau a lou tino.
samoan_glosses: Na tauto Ieova ia=to take an oath the LORD to; Tavita=David; i le faamaoni,=in the faithful; e le 'alo ese ai=shall not Son away; o ia,=he; Ou te faanofoina=I will a secondary wife intro-; i lou nofoālii so=in your throne so; le fanau=the born
kjv_text: The LORD hath sworn in truth unto David; he will not turn from it; Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy throne.
issue: Theological term שׁוּב (shuv) = turn, return, repent not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ia outou sisii i luga o outou lima i le malumalu, ma ia outou faamanū atu ia Ieova.
samoan_glosses: Ia outou sisii i luga=to you (all) to lift in above; o outou lima=you (all) five; i le malumalu,=in the temple; ma ia outou=and to you (all); faamanū atu ia=faamanū to; Ieova.=the LORD
kjv_text: Lift up your hands in the sanctuary, and bless the LORD.
issue: Theological term קָדוֹשׁ (qadosh) = holy, set apart, sacred not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: e fai o latou taliga, a e le faalogo; e leai foi se mānava i o latou gutu.
samoan_glosses: e fai o latou=to make of they; taliga,=ear; a=but; e le faalogo;=shall not hearken; e leai foi=there is not also; se mānava=a mānava; i o latou=in of they; gutu.=mouth
kjv_text: They have ears, but they hear not; neither is there any breath in their mouths.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E avae e ia o ausa mai tulu'iga o le lalolagi; na te faia le uila atoa ma le ua; na te aumaia le matagi mai mea e teu ai ana oloa.
samoan_glosses: E avae e=to take away by; ia=to; o ausa mai tulu'iga=ausa from anointing; o le lalolagi;=the earth; na te faia=he made; le uila=the uila; atoa ma le=and the; ua;=ua;
kjv_text: He causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings for the rain; he bringeth the wind out of his treasuries.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ou te vivii atu ia te oe, auā na faia a'u e ala ai ona matautia ma ofo tele ai; o mea na e faia, o mea mamana ia; ua ou matuā iloa e a'u nei mea .
samoan_glosses: Ou te vivii atu=I will praised; ia te oe,=unto you; auā=for; na faia a'u=made me; e ala ai=to way; ona matautia=and fearful; ma ofo tele ai;=and n great; o mea=thing
kjv_text: I will praise thee; for I am fearfully and wonderfully made: marvellous are thy works; and that my soul knoweth right well.
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ieova e lo'u Atua, ia e silasila mai ma tali mai ia te au; ia e faamalamalamaina o'u mata, ne'i ou moe i le oti;
samoan_glosses: Ieova=the LORD; e lo'u Atua,=to my God; ia e silasila mai=to to looked; ma tali mai ia te au;=and respond from me; ia e faamalamalamaina=to to give light; o'u mata,=my eye; ne'i ou moe=now I sleep; i le oti;=in the die
kjv_text: Consider and hear me, O LORD my God: lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death;
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ou te pese atu ia Ieova, auā ua agalelei mai o ia ia te au.
samoan_glosses: Ou te pese atu=I will a song; ia Ieova,=to the LORD; auā=for; ua agalelei mai=kindness from; o ia ia te au.=he unto me
kjv_text: I will sing unto the LORD, because he hath dealt bountifully with me.
issue: Theological term בְּרִית (berit) = binding covenant agreement not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E moni, o e maiotonu, e vivii atu i latou i lou suafa; e nonofo i ou luma o e ua sa'o a latou amio.
samoan_glosses: E moni,=verily/truly; o=o; e maiotonu,=by maiotonu; e vivii atu=to praised; i latou=them; i lou suafa;=in your name; e nonofo=to dwell; i ou luma=before thee
kjv_text: Surely the righteous shall give thanks unto thy name: the upright shall dwell in thy presence.
issue: Theological term מִשְׁפָּט (mishpat) = justice, judgment, ordinance not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ieova e, aua e te avatu mea e manao i ai le tagata amio leaga; aua ne'i oo lana togafiti, nei faamaualuga i latou. Selā.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; aua=for; e te avatu mea=by give away thing; e manao=to desire; i ai=therein; le tagata amio leaga;=the man iniquity; aua ne'i oo=for now oo; lana togafiti,=his heal
kjv_text: Grant not, O LORD, the desires of the wicked: further not his wicked device; lest they exalt themselves. Selah.
issue: Theological term יְשׁוּעָה (yeshuah) = salvation, deliverance not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Na ou tepa i le itu taumatau, ma ou vaavaai, a ua leai se tasi na fia iloa a'u; ua leai lava se 'alofaga ia te au; ua leai se tasi na te sailia a'u ia ou ola.
samoan_glosses: Na ou tepa=I to look towards; i le itu taumatau,=on the side right hand; ma ou vaavaai,=and I watch; a=but; ua leai se=there was no; tasi=one; na fia iloa a'u;=desired to know me; ua leai lava=there was not indeed
kjv_text: I looked on my right hand, and beheld, but there was no man that would know me: refuge failed me; no man cared for my soul.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ieova e, na ou alaga atu ia te oe; na a'u faapea atu, O oe lava lo'u malu, o lo'u tofi i le nuu o tagata ola.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; na ou alaga atu ia te oe;=I cried unto you; na a'u faapea atu,=me thus; O oe lava=you indeed; lo'u malu,=my female tattoo; o lo'u tofi=my lot; i le nuu=in the land; o tagata ola.=of men life
kjv_text: I cried unto thee, O LORD: I said, Thou art my refuge and my portion in the land of the living.
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ieova e, ia e vave tali mai ia te au, o le a faauma lo'u nei ola; aua le 'alo ou fofoga ia te au, ne'i tusa a'u ma tagata e o ifo i le lua.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; ia e vave=to to quick; tali mai ia te au,=respond from me; o le a faauma=what finish; lo'u nei ola;=my this life; aua=for; le 'alo ou fofoga=the Son I face; ia te au,=unto me
kjv_text: Hear me speedily, O LORD: my spirit faileth: hide not thy face from me, lest I be like unto them that go down into the pit.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E alofa fua mai ma alofa mutimuti vale mai Ieova; e telegese lona toasa, e tele foi lona alofa.
samoan_glosses: E alofa fua mai=to love fruit from; ma alofa mutimuti vale=and love mutimuti foolish; mai Ieova;=from the LORD; e telegese=by telegese; lona toasa,=his wrath; e tele foi=to great also; lona alofa.=his love
kjv_text: The LORD is gracious, and full of compassion; slow to anger, and of great mercy.
issue: Theological term בְּרִית (berit) = binding covenant agreement not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: O loo finagalo Ieova i e matatau ia te ia, o e faamoemoe i lona alofa.
samoan_glosses: O loo finagalo Ieova=is (progressive) a chiefs will or desire the LORD; i=in; e matatau ia te ia,=to fear to him; o=o; e faamoemoe=to hope; i lona alofa.=in his love
kjv_text: The LORD taketh pleasure in them that fear him, in those that hope in his mercy.
issue: Theological term בְּרִית (berit) = binding covenant agreement not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E tŭlei mai lana afioga, ona liu suāvai ai lea o ia mea; e faaagi mai e ia lona matagi, ona tafe mai ai lea o vai.
samoan_glosses: E tŭlei mai=by tŭlei from; lana afioga,=his word (divine); ona liu suāvai ai lea=and liu suāvai then then; o ia mea;=he thing; e faaagi mai=by faaagi from; e ia=by him; lona matagi,=his wind; ona tafe mai ai lea=and to flow from then then
kjv_text: He sendeth out his word, and melteth them: he causeth his wind to blow, and the waters flow.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: o le afi ma le uato'a, o le kiona ma le ausa; o le matagi e afā ai, o loo faia lana afioga.
samoan_glosses: o le afi=the fire; ma le uato'a,=and the uato'a; o le kiona=the kiona; ma le ausa;=and the ausa; o le matagi=the wind; e afā ai,=to cyclone; o loo faia=is (progressive) made; lana afioga.=his word (divine)
kjv_text: Fire, and hail; snow, and vapour; stormy wind fulfilling his word:
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ia outou vivii atu ia Ioa. Ia outou vivii atu i le Atua i lona malumalu; ia vivii atu outou ia te ia i le va nimonimo o i ai lona malosi.
samoan_glosses: Ia outou vivii atu ia=to you (all) praised to; Ioa.=name; Ia outou vivii atu i le Atua i lona=to you (all) praised in the God in his; malumalu;=temple; ia vivii atu outou=to praised you (all); ia te ia=to him; i le va nimonimo=in the expanse firmament; o=o
kjv_text: Praise ye the LORD. Praise God in his sanctuary: praise him in the firmament of his power.
issue: Theological term קָדוֹשׁ (qadosh) = holy, set apart, sacred not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ua puputa lava o latou tino; o loo tautalatala faasausili o latou gutu.
samoan_glosses: Ua puputa lava=a species of banana indeed; o latou tino;=they body; o loo tautalatala faasausili=is (progressive) speak haughty; o latou gutu.=they mouth
kjv_text: They are inclosed in their own fat: with their mouth they speak proudly.
issue: Theological term גָּאוֹן (gaon) = pride, majesty, swelling, thicket not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Na faia e ia o le pouliuli ma mea e lilo ai o ia; o lona apitaga ua siomia ai o ia, o le vai pouliuli, o ao uliuli o le lagi.
samoan_glosses: Na faia e=made you; ia=to; o le pouliuli=the darkness; ma mea=and thing; e lilo ai=to secret; o ia;=he; o lona apitaga=his a tempoiary; ua siomia ai=surrounded
kjv_text: He made darkness his secret place; his pavilion round about him were dark waters and thick clouds of the skies.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ua ia aapa mai lona aao mai luga, ma tago ia te ua, ua ia faaea ia te au ai vai e tele.
samoan_glosses: Ua ia aapa mai=to reached out from; lona aao mai luga,=his hand (extended) from above; ma tago ia te=and reach unto; ua,=ua,; ua ia faaea ia te au=to faaea unto me; ai vai e tele.=water to great
kjv_text: He sent from above, he took me, he drew me out of many waters.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Auā ua ou tausia ala o Ieova, ma a'u le lafoai faalematau i lo'u Atua.
samoan_glosses: Auā=for; ua ou tausia=I nursed; ala o Ieova,=way the LORD; ma a'u=and me; le lafoai faalematau=the forsaken faalematau; i lo'u Atua.=in my God
kjv_text: For I have kept the ways of the LORD, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
issue: Theological term צֶדֶק (tsedeq) = righteousness, justice not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ua faalauteleina e oe le mea ou te savali ai, ne'i mapeva o'u vae.
samoan_glosses: Ua faalauteleina e=made wide by; oe=you; le mea ou te savali ai,=the thing I will walk; ne'i mapeva o'u vae.=now mapeva my foot
kjv_text: Thou hast enlarged my steps under me, that my feet did not slip.

samoan_text: Na e laveai ia te au ai fefinauaiga ma le nuu; ua e tofia a'u ia ou pule i nuu ese; e anauna mai ia te au le nuu na ou le iloa.
samoan_glosses: Na e laveai ia te au=you delivered unto me; ai fefinauaiga=dispute; ma le nuu;=and the nation; ua e tofia a'u=you chosen me; ia ou pule i=to I rule in; nuu ese;=nation away; e anauna mai ia te au=by anauna from me; le nuu=the nation
kjv_text: Thou hast delivered me from the strivings of the people; and thou hast made me the head of the heathen: a people whom I have not known shall serve me.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: A latou lagona, ona latou faalogo ai lea ia te au; e faatagā vivii mai ia te au tagata ese.
samoan_glosses: A latou lagona,=but they to understand; ona latou faalogo ai lea=and they hearken then then; ia te au;=unto me; e faatagā vivii=by faatagā praised; mai ia te au=from me; tagata ese.=man away
kjv_text: As soon as they hear of me, they shall obey me: the strangers shall submit themselves unto me.
issue: Theological term שָׁחָה (shachah) = bow down, worship, prostrate not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: O le Atua lea o loo faia le taui ma sui mo a'u; o le ua faatoilaloina foi le nuu ia te au.
samoan_glosses: O le Atua lea=of God; o loo faia=is (progressive) made; le taui=the a reward; ma sui=and to dilute; mo a'u;=for me; o le=the; ua faatoilaloina foi=spared also; le nuu ia te au.=the nation unto me

samoan_text: Ona luluina ai lea ma mafuie le lalolagi; ua gatete foi faavae o mauga, ua luluina i latou, auā na toasa lava o ia.
samoan_glosses: Ona luluina ai lea=and shaken then then; ma mafuie=and mafuie; le lalolagi;=the earth; ua gatete foi=tremble also; faavae o mauga,=foundation of mountain; ua luluina=shaken; i latou,=them; auā=for
kjv_text: Then the earth shook and trembled; the foundations also of the hills moved and were shaken, because he was wroth.

samoan_text: E silisili ona lelei i le auro, o le auro e sili ona lelei e tele; e sili foi ona suamalie i lo le meli, o le meli ua sisina ifo.
samoan_glosses: E silisili=to very much; ona lelei=and good; i le auro,=in the gold; o le auro=the gold; e sili=to most; ona lelei e tele;=and good to great; e sili foi=to most also; ona suamalie=and - sweet

samoan_text: E silisili ona lelei i le auro, o le auro e sili ona lelei e tele; e sili foi ona suamalie i lo le meli, o le meli ua sisina ifo.
samoan_glosses: E silisili=to very much; ona lelei=and good; i le auro,=in the gold; o le auro=the gold; e sili=to most; ona lelei e tele;=and good to great; e sili foi=to most also; ona suamalie=and - sweet

samoan_text: E silisili ona lelei i le auro, o le auro e sili ona lelei e tele; e sili foi ona suamalie i lo le meli, o le meli ua sisina ifo.
samoan_glosses: E silisili=to very much; ona lelei=and good; i le auro,=in the gold; o le auro=the gold; e sili=to most; ona lelei e tele;=and good to great; e sili foi=to most also; ona suamalie=and - sweet

samoan_text: E silisili ona lelei i le auro, o le auro e sili ona lelei e tele; e sili foi ona suamalie i lo le meli, o le meli ua sisina ifo.
samoan_glosses: E silisili=to very much; ona lelei=and good; i le auro,=in the gold; o le auro=the gold; e sili=to most; ona lelei e tele;=and good to great; e sili foi=to most also; ona suamalie=and - sweet

samoan_text: O loo ta'u atu le upu e le tasi aso i le tasi aso, o loo faailoa foi le poto e le tasi po i le tasi po.
samoan_glosses: O loo ta'u atu=is/are (progressive) told; le upu=the word; e le tasi aso=shall not one day; i le tasi aso,=in the one day; o loo faailoa foi=is (progressive) revealed also; le poto=the wise; e le tasi po=shall not one night; i le tasi po.=in the one night
kjv_text: Day unto day uttereth speech, and night unto night sheweth knowledge.

samoan_text: E le faapea o e amio leaga; a e pei o aputi o saito i latou ua lelea i le matagi.
samoan_glosses: E le faapea=shall not thus; o=o; e amio leaga;=to iniquity; a=but; e pei o aputi=like aputi; o saito=wheat; i latou=them; ua lelea=to be carried away by the
kjv_text: The ungodly are not so: but are like the chaff which the wind driveth away.

samoan_text: Ia manatua e ia au taulaga uma i mea e 'ai, ia talia foi au taulaga mu. Selā.
samoan_glosses: Ia manatua e=to remember by; ia au taulaga=to me offering; uma=all; i mea=in thing; e 'ai,=to eat; ia talia foi=to received also; au taulaga mu.=me burnt offerings; Selā.=Selah

samoan_text: Ia foaiina e ia ia te oe e tusa ma lou lava loto, ma ia faataunuuina lau pule uma lava.
samoan_glosses: Ia foaiina e=to given by; ia ia te oe=to him you; e tusa ma lou=according to your; lava loto,=indeed heart; ma ia faataunuuina=and to fulfilled; lau pule uma lava.=your rule all indeed

samoan_text: O loo ta'uta'u nisi i kariota, ma nisi i solofanua; a o i tatou ua tatou ta'uta'u le suafa o Ieova lo tatou Atua.
samoan_glosses: O loo ta'uta'u nisi=is (progressive) to hang up some; i kariota,=in chariot; ma nisi=and some; i solofanua;=in horse; a o=but of; i tatou=in we (incl); ua tatou ta'uta'u=we (incl) to hang up; le suafa o Ieova lo tatou Atua.=the name the LORD our God
kjv_text: Some trust in chariots, and some in horses: but we will remember the name of the LORD our God.
issue: Theological term קָדוֹשׁ (qadosh) = holy, set apart, sacred not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: O loo ta'uta'u nisi i kariota, ma nisi i solofanua; a o i tatou ua tatou ta'uta'u le suafa o Ieova lo tatou Atua.
samoan_glosses: O loo ta'uta'u nisi=is (progressive) to hang up some; i kariota,=in chariot; ma nisi=and some; i solofanua;=in horse; a o=but of; i tatou=in we (incl); ua tatou ta'uta'u=we (incl) to hang up; le suafa o Ieova lo tatou Atua.=the name the LORD our God

samoan_text: Auā ua e tuuina ia te ia le manuia e oo i le faavavau; ua e faaolioliina o ia i le fiafia i ou luma.
samoan_glosses: Auā=for; ua e tuuina ia te ia=you given to him; le manuia=the peace; e oo=it shall come to pass; i le faavavau;=in the everlasting; ua e faaolioliina=you made rejoice; o ia=he; i le fiafia=in the competition
kjv_text: For thou hast made him most blessed for ever: thou hast made him exceeding glad with thy countenance.

samoan_text: E maua e lou aao o i latou uma o e ita ia te oe; e maua lava e lou aao taumatau o e 'ino'ino ia te oe.
samoan_glosses: E maua=to received; e lou aao=by your hand (extended); o=o; i latou uma=them all; o=o; e ita ia te oe;=to angry unto you; e maua lava=to received indeed; e lou aao taumatau=by your hand (extended) right hand
kjv_text: Thine hand shall find out all thine enemies: thy right hand shall find out those that hate thee.

samoan_text: Na tuuina atu a'u ia te oe talu lo'u fanau mai; o oe lava lo'u Atua talu ina ou i le manava o lo'u tinā.
samoan_glosses: Na tuuina atu a'u=given / delivered me; ia te oe=unto you; talu=since; lo'u fanau mai;=my born; o oe lava=you indeed; lo'u Atua talu=my God since; ina ou=so that I; i le manava=in the womb
kjv_text: I was cast upon thee from the womb: thou art my God from my mother's belly.
issue: Theological term בָּטַח (batach) = trust, be confident, feel secure not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E auauna ia te ia le fanau; e ta'ua i latou e fai ma tupulaga mo le Alii.
samoan_glosses: E auauna ia te ia=to servant to him; le fanau;=the born; e ta'ua=by us (two, incl); i latou=them; e fai ma tupulaga=to be generation; mo le Alii.=for the Lord
kjv_text: A seed shall serve him; it shall be accounted to the Lord for a generation.
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Na faatuatua ia te oe o matou tamā; na latou faatuatua, ua e laveai foi ia te i latou.
samoan_glosses: Na faatuatua ia te oe=faith unto you; o matou tamā;=we (excl) father; na latou faatuatua,=they faith; ua e laveai foi=you delivered also; ia te i latou.=unto them

issue: Theological term קָדוֹשׁ (qadosh) = holy, set apart, sacred not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E moni e mulimuli mai ia te au le agalelei ma le alofa i aso uma o lo'u ola; ou te nofo foi i le fale o Ieova i aso e tele lava.
samoan_glosses: E moni=verily/truly; e mulimuli mai ia te au=to follow after from me; le agalelei=the kindness; ma le alofa=and the love; i aso uma=all the days; o lo'u ola;=my life; ou te nofo foi=I will dwell also; i le fale=in the house
kjv_text: Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life: and I will dwell in the house of the LORD for ever.

samoan_text: E mau o ia i le manuia; o lana fanau foi e fai mo latou le nuu.
samoan_glosses: E mau o ia=to opinion he; i le manuia;=in the peace; o lana fanau foi=his born also; e fai=to make; mo latou=for they; le nuu.=the nation
kjv_text: His soul shall dwell at ease; and his seed shall inherit the earth.
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ieova e, ia e manatua lou alofa mutimuti vale ma lou agalelei; auā talu mai le vavau lava ia.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; ia e manatua=to to remember; lou alofa mutimuti vale=your love mutimuti foolish; ma lou agalelei;=and your kindness; auā talu mai le vavau=for since from the ancient times; lava ia.=indeed to

samoan_text: Aua e te manatua a'u agasala ina o ou taulealea, ma o'u solitulafono; a e tusa ma lou alofa ia e manatua mai a'u, Ieova e, ona o lou agalelei.
samoan_glosses: Aua=for; e te manatua a'u=by remember me; agasala=sin; ina o ou taulealea,=so that of I young man; ma o'u solitulafono;=and my transgression; a=but; e tusa ma lou alofa=according to your love; ia e manatua=to to remember

samoan_text: Ieova e, ou te fiafia i le mea e tu ai lou maota, o le mea foi e afio ai lou mamalu.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; ou te fiafia=I will competition; i le mea=in the thing; e tu ai=to stand; lou maota,=your the name of a tree; o le mea foi=the thing also; e afio ai=to dwell (respectful); lou mamalu.=your glory

samoan_text: E ui lava ina to lauapi se itu taua e tau ma a'u, e le fefe ai lo'u loto; e ui ina siitia se taua ia te au, ou te loto tele lava i lea mea.
samoan_glosses: E ui lava=to although indeed; ina to lauapi=so that to plant a war lod|^ing houfe; se itu taua=a side precious; e tau=to season; ma a'u,=and me; e le fefe ai=shall not get scared; lo'u loto;=my heart; e ui=to although

samoan_text: O Ieova, o lo latou malosi ia, o ia foi o le malosi e ola ai le na ia faauuina.
samoan_glosses: O Ieova,=the LORD; o lo latou malosi=their strong; ia,=to; o ia foi=he also; o le malosi=the strong; e ola ai=to life; le=the; na ia faauuina.=to anointed

samoan_text: Auā o lona toasa ua se emo o le mata lea, a o lona alofa tunoa o le ola lava lea; e afe ane le tagi i le afiafi, a o le alaga fiafia i le taeao.
samoan_glosses: Auā=for; o lona toasa=his wrath; ua=ua; se emo=a emo; o le mata lea,=the eye; a=but; o lona alofa tunoa=his/her grace/mercy; o le ola=the life
kjv_text: For his anger endureth but a moment; in his favour is life: weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning.

samoan_text: Ua faalumaina a'u e i latou uma o e ita mai ia te au, a e sili lava i latou matou te tuaoi; ua avea a'u ma faamatau i e sa matou masani; ua sosola ese ia te au o e iloa mai a'u i fafo.
samoan_glosses: Ua faalumaina a'u=to stigmatize me; e=by; i latou uma=them all; o=o; e ita mai ia te au,=to angry from me; a=but; e sili lava=to most indeed; i latou matou=them we (excl)
kjv_text: I was a reproach among all mine enemies, but especially among my neighbours, and a fear to mine acquaintance: they that did see me without fled from me.

samoan_text: Ieova e, aua ne'i famaina lava a'u, auā ou te valaau ia te oe; ia faamaina o e fai amio leaga, ia faalologo i latou i seoli.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; aua ne'i famaina=for now famaina; lava a'u,=indeed me; auā ou te valaau=for I will call; ia te oe;=unto you; ia faamaina o=to to put to shame of; e fai amio leaga,=to make iniquity; ia faalologo i latou=to to hold one's tongue them
kjv_text: Let me not be ashamed, O LORD; for I have called upon thee: let the wicked be ashamed, and let them be silent in the grave.

samoan_text: Ua tele lava lou agalelei ua teua e oe mo e ua matatau ia te oe, ua e faia i luma o le fanau a tagata mo e faatuatua ia te oe.

samoan_text: Ua tele lava lou agalelei ua teua e oe mo e ua matatau ia te oe, ua e faia i luma o le fanau a tagata mo e faatuatua ia te oe.
samoan_glosses: Ua tele lava=great indeed; lou agalelei=your kindness; ua teua e=teua by; oe=you; mo=for; e ua matatau=by feared; ia te oe,=unto you; ua e faia=you made

samoan_text: Ia e liliu mai ou fofoga ia te au; ia e vave laveai ia te au; ia fai oe mo'u papa malosi, o le 'olo mauga ou te ola ai.
samoan_glosses: Ia e liliu=to to turn; mai ou fofoga=from I face; ia te au;=unto me; ia e vave=to to quick; laveai ia te au;=deliver unto me; ia fai oe=to make you; mo'u papa malosi,=many rock strong; o le 'olo mauga ou te ola ai.=the fortress mountain I will life

samoan_text: O a'u foi na ou fai atu i lo'u polepole vale, Ua faateaina a'u ai ou luma; a e peitai ua e faafofoga mai i le leo o la'u aioi atu i lo'u alaga atu ia te oe.
samoan_glosses: O a'u foi=I am also; na ou fai atu=I said to; i lo'u polepole vale,=in my the portion of food for foolish; Ua faateaina a'u=faateaina me; ai ou luma;=I before; a=but; e peitai=to however; ua e faafofoga mai=you listen from

samoan_text: Ou te 'ino'ino i e ua anaana i mea faatauvaa ma le pepelo; a e ou te faatuatua lava ia Ieova.

samoan_text: Ou te 'ino'ino i e ua anaana i mea faatauvaa ma le pepelo; a e ou te faatuatua lava ia Ieova.

samoan_glosses: Ua e le=you the; tuuina foi=given also; a'u=me; i le pule=in the rule; a=but; e ita mai ia te au;=to angry from me; ua e faatuina=you built; o'u vae=my foot

samoan_text: ina ia laveai ia te i latou ai le oti, ina ia ola foi i latou i le oge.

samoan_text: E oge mea leona taanoa, ma latou fia aai; a e e leai se mea lelei e mativa ai i latou o e saili ia Ieova.

kjv_text: The LORD redeemeth the soul of his servants: and none of them that trust in him shall be desolate.
issue: Theological term צַדִּיק (tsaddiq) = righteous one not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ia avea i latou ia faapei o aputi o saito ona lelea i le matagi; ia tuleia i latou e le agelu a Ieova.
samoan_glosses: Ia avea i latou=to become them; ia faapei o=to like of; aputi o saito=aputi of wheat; ona lelea=and to be carried away by the; i le matagi;=in the wind; ia tuleia i latou=to tuleia them; e le agelu=shall not angel; a Ieova.=of the LORD
kjv_text: Let them be as chaff before the wind: and let the angel of the LORD chase them.
issue: Theological term רוּחַ (ruach) = spirit, wind, breath of God not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Auā na latou natia fua lo latou upega ia maua ai a'u i le lua, na latou elia fua lava ia ou oti ai.
samoan_glosses: Auā=for; na latou natia=they hid; fua lo latou upega=fruit their net; ia maua ai=to received; a'u=me; i le lua,=in the two; na latou elia=they Elijah; fua lava ia=fruit indeed to
kjv_text: For without cause have they hid for me their net in a pit, which without cause they have digged for my soul.
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Aua ne'i oo ia te au le vae o lē faamaualuga; aua foi ne'i tulieseina a'u e lima o e ua amio leaga.
samoan_glosses: Aua ne'i oo=for now oo; ia te au=unto me; le vae o lē faamaualuga;=the foot of not exalted; aua foi=for also; ne'i tulieseina a'u=now tulieseina me; e lima o=by five of; e ua amio leaga.=by iniquity
kjv_text: Let not the foot of pride come against me, and let not the hand of the wicked remove me.
issue: Theological term חֶסֶד (chesed) = covenant loyalty, steadfast love not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ua pauu e ua fai amio leaga i lea lava mea; ua tuleia lava i latou, ua le toe mafai ai ona tutū i luga.
samoan_glosses: Ua pauu=fell; e ua fai amio leaga=by made iniquity; i lea=in; lava mea;=indeed thing; ua tuleia lava=tuleia indeed; i latou,=them; ua=ua; le toe mafai ai=the again able
kjv_text: There are the workers of iniquity fallen: they are cast down, and shall not be able to rise.
issue: Theological term גָּאוֹן (gaon) = pride, majesty, swelling, thicket not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ieova e, o i le lagi lou alofa; ua oo lava i ao lou faamaoni.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; o=o; i le lagi=in heaven; lou alofa;=your love; ua oo lava=it came to pass indeed; i ao=in day; lou faamaoni.=your faithful
kjv_text: Thy mercy, O LORD, is in the heavens; and thy faithfulness reacheth unto the clouds.
issue: Theological term עָוֹן (avon) = iniquity, guilt, punishment not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E pei o le atu mauga o le Atua lau amiotonu; o le moana sausau au faamasino; Ieova e, ua ola ia te oe tagata atoa ma manu.
samoan_glosses: E pei o le atu mauga=like the mountain; o le Atua=of God; lau amiotonu;=your righteous; o le moana sausau au=the deep sprinkle me; faamasino;=judge; Ieova e,=O LORD!; ua ola ia te oe=life unto you; tagata=man
kjv_text: Thy righteousness is like the great mountains; thy judgments are a great deep: O LORD, thou preservest man and beast.
issue: Theological term חֶסֶד (chesed) = covenant loyalty, steadfast love not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Le Atua e, e maeu lava lou alofa, o le mea lea ua faatuatua ai tagata i le paolo o ou apaau.
samoan_glosses: Le Atua e,=God by; e maeu lava=by maeu indeed; lou alofa,=your love; o le mea lea=therefore; ua faatuatua ai=faith; tagata=man; i le paolo=in the paolo; o ou apaau.=I a wing
kjv_text: How excellent is thy lovingkindness, O God! therefore the children of men put their trust under the shadow of thy wings.
issue: Theological term צֶדֶק (tsedeq) = righteousness, justice not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E malilie i latou i mea lololo ua i lou fale; e te faainumia foi i latou i le vaitafe o au mea faafiafia.
samoan_glosses: E malilie=by malilie; i latou=them; i mea lololo=in thing lololo; ua=ua; i lou fale;=in your house; e te faainumia foi=by faainumia also; i latou=them; i le vaitafe=in the river
kjv_text: They shall be abundantly satisfied with the fatness of thy house; and thou shalt make them drink of the river of thy pleasures.
issue: Theological term חֶסֶד (chesed) = covenant loyalty, steadfast love not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: A ua tagata ola i latou o e ita mai ia te au, ua malolosi i latou; ua faatoateleina o e 'ino'ino fua mai ia te au.
samoan_glosses: A=but; ua tagata ola=man life; i latou=them; o=o; e ita mai ia te au,=to angry from me; ua malolosi=mighty; i latou;=them; ua faatoateleina o=made many of
kjv_text: But mine enemies are lively, and they are strong: and they that hate me wrongfully are multiplied.
issue: Theological term חַטָּאת (chattat) = sin, sin offering not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ieova e, ia e faafofoga mai i la'u tatalo, ma liliu mai ou fofoga i la'u alaga; aua ne'i e le tali mai i la'u tagi; auā ou te se tagata ese a'u i ou luma ma le āumau, e pei o o'u tamā uma lava.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; ia e faafofoga=to to listen; mai=from; i la'u tatalo,=in my pray; ma liliu mai ou fofoga=and turn from I face; i la'u alaga;=in my cry; aua ne'i=for now; e le tali=shall not respond
kjv_text: Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear unto my cry; hold not thy peace at my tears: for I am a stranger with thee, and a sojourner, as all my fathers were.
issue: Theological term עָוֹן (avon) = iniquity, guilt, punishment not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: A o oe, Ieova e, ua fai oe mo'u talita; o lo'u mamalu foi , ma lē faaeaina lo'u ulu.
samoan_glosses: A o oe,=but of you; Ieova e,=O LORD!; ua fai oe=made you; mo'u talita;=many shield; o lo'u mamalu foi=my glory also; ,=,; ma lē faaeaina=and not faaeaina; lo'u ulu.=my head
kjv_text: But thou, O LORD, art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head.
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Na ou taoto lava a'u, ma ou moe; na ou ala, auā ua lagolagoina mai Ieova ia te au.
samoan_glosses: Na ou taoto lava a'u,=I lay down indeed me; ma ou moe;=and I sleep; na ou ala,=I way; auā=for; ua lagolagoina mai=lagolagoina from; Ieova ia te au.=the LORD unto me
kjv_text: I laid me down and slept; I awaked; for the LORD sustained me.
issue: Theological term קָדוֹשׁ (qadosh) = holy, set apart, sacred not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ieova e, aua e te taofi e oe lou alofa mutimuti vale ia te au; ia leoleoina pea lava a'u e lou alofa ma lou faamaoni.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; aua=for; e te taofi=by hold; e oe=to you; lou alofa mutimuti vale=your love mutimuti foolish; ia te au;=unto me; ia leoleoina pea lava a'u=to guarded continually indeed me; e lou alofa=by your love
kjv_text: Withhold not thou thy tender mercies from me, O LORD: let thy lovingkindness and thy truth continually preserve me.
issue: Theological term צֶדֶק (tsedeq) = righteousness, justice not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ieova e, aua e te taofi e oe lou alofa mutimuti vale ia te au; ia leoleoina pea lava a'u e lou alofa ma lou faamaoni.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; aua=for; e te taofi=by hold; e oe=to you; lou alofa mutimuti vale=your love mutimuti foolish; ia te au;=unto me; ia leoleoina pea lava a'u=to guarded continually indeed me; e lou alofa=by your love

samoan_text: Auā ua siosiomia a'u i mea leaga e le mafaitaulia; ua maua mai a'u e a'u agasala, o lea ou te le mafaia ai ona vaai; ua sili ia ona tele i o'u lauulu; ua matuā faavaivai ai lo'u loto.
samoan_glosses: Auā=for; ua siosiomia a'u=surrounded me; i mea leaga=in thing evil; e le mafaitaulia;=by the mafaitaulia; ua maua mai a'u=received from me; e a'u agasala,=to me sin; o lea=therefore; ou te=I will
kjv_text: For innumerable evils have compassed me about: mine iniquities have taken hold upon me, so that I am not able to look up; they are more than the hairs of mine head: therefore my heart faileth me.
issue: Theological term חֶסֶד (chesed) = covenant loyalty, steadfast love not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Amuia le tagata o lē faatuatua ia Ieova, a e le faasaga i e faamaualuluga, ma e ua liliu ane i mea pepelo.
samoan_glosses: Amuia=blessed; le tagata o lē faatuatua=the man of not faith; ia Ieova,=to the LORD; a=but; e le faasaga=shall not face; i=in; e faamaualuluga,=to make most high; ma=and

samoan_text: Ieova e, lo'u Atua, e tele lava mea ua e faia, o ou vavega ma ou manatu ia te i matou, e le mafai ona laulauina atu ai ia te oe; ou te fia faailoa ma tautala i ai, a e le mafaitaulia lava.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; lo'u Atua,=my God; e tele lava mea=to great indeed thing; ua e faia,=you made; o ou vavega=I a wonder; ma ou manatu=and I thought; ia te i matou,=unto in we (excl); e le mafai ona=shall not able then
kjv_text: Many, O LORD my God, are thy wonderful works which thou hast done, and thy thoughts which are to us-ward: they cannot be reckoned up in order unto thee: if I would declare and speak of them, they are
issue: Theological term בָּטַח (batach) = trust, be confident, feel secure not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ona ou fai ane ai lea, Faauta, ou te alu atu;

issue: Theological term זֶבַח (zevach) = sacrifice, slaughter not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ona ou fai ane ai lea, Faauta, ou te alu atu;

samoan_text: A o oe, Ieova e, ia e alofa mai ia te au, ma faatu ia te au, ina ia a'u taui atu ia te i latou.
samoan_glosses: A o oe,=but of you; Ieova e,=O LORD!; ia e alofa=to to love; mai ia te au,=from me; ma faatu ia te au,=and pitch unto me; ina ia a'u=so that me; taui atu ia te=a reward unto; i latou.=them

issue: Theological term בָּטַח (batach) = trust, be confident, feel secure not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Lo'u Atua e, ua faanoanoa lo'u agaga i totonu ia te au; o le mea lea ou te manatunatu ai ia te oe i le nuu o Ioritana, ma le atu Heremoni, ma le mauga itiiti.
samoan_glosses: Lo'u Atua e,=my God by; ua faanoanoa=mourned; lo'u agaga=my spirit; i totonu ia te au;=in inside unto me; o le mea lea=therefore; ou te manatunatu ai=I will meditate; ia te oe=unto you; i le nuu=in the land
kjv_text: O my God, my soul is cast down within me: therefore will I remember thee from the land of Jordan, and of the Hermonites, from the hill Mizar.

samoan_text: Ou te fai atu i le Atua lo'u papa, Se a le mea ua e faagalogalo ai ia te au? Se a le mea ou te fealuai ai ma le eleelea, ona o le saua mai o le ita mai?
samoan_glosses: Ou te fai atu=I will said to; i le Atua=in the God; lo'u papa,=my rock; Se a le mea=what / why; ua e faagalogalo ai=you to forget; ia te au?=unto me; Se a le mea ou te fealuai ai=what / why I will walk about; ma le eleelea,=and the eleelea

issue: Theological term חֶסֶד (chesed) = covenant loyalty, steadfast love not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E lei tuumuli o matou loto; e lei liliu ese foi o matou vae i lou ala;
samoan_glosses: E lei tuumuli=by not tuumuli; o matou loto;=we (excl) heart; e lei liliu ese foi=by not turn away also; o matou vae=we (excl) foot; i lou ala;=in your way

samoan_text: O oe lava matou te tulei ai i e ita mai ia te i matou; o lou suafa foi matou te soli ai i e laga mai ia te i matou.
samoan_glosses: O oe lava matou=you indeed we (excl); te tulei ai=to push; i=in; e ita mai ia te=to angry from; i matou;=in we (excl); o lou suafa foi=your name also; matou te soli ai=we (excl) to tread upon; i=in

issue: Theological term יְשׁוּעָה (yeshuah) = salvation, deliverance not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Auā ua e faaola lava ia te i matou i e ita mai ia te i matou; ua e faamasiasi foi i e 'ino'ino mai ia te i matou.
samoan_glosses: Auā=for; ua e faaola lava ia te=you saved indeed unto; i matou=in we (excl); i=in; e ita mai ia te=to angry from; i matou;=in we (excl); ua e faamasiasi foi=you to put to shame also; i=in
kjv_text: But thou hast saved us from our enemies, and hast put them to shame that hated us.
issue: Theological term בָּטַח (batach) = trust, be confident, feel secure not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E suia ou tamā i ou alo; e te tofia i latou e fai ma alii sili i le lalolagi uma.
samoan_glosses: E suia ou tamā=by suia I father; i ou alo;=in I Son; e te tofia=by chosen; i latou=them; e fai ma alii sili=to be Lord most; i le lalolagi uma.=on the earth all

samoan_text: E maamaai au u fanafana; e pauu ai tagata ia te oe; e tuia ai loto o e faia le Tupu mo latou fili.
samoan_glosses: E maamaai au=by maamaai me; u fanafana;=u arrow; e pauu ai=to fell; tagata ia te oe;=man unto you; e tuia ai=by tuia; loto o=heart of; e faia=to made; le Tupu=the king

samoan_text: E maamaai au u fanafana; e pauu ai tagata ia te oe; e tuia ai loto o e faia le Tupu mo latou fili.

issue: Theological term צֶדֶק (tsedeq) = righteousness, justice not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Le Atua e, e pei o lou suafa, e faapea lou viiga ona oo i tulu'iga o le lalolagi; ua tumu lou aao taumatau i le amiotonu.
samoan_glosses: Le Atua e,=God by; e pei o lou suafa,=like your name; e faapea=saying; lou viiga=your praise; ona oo=and oo; i tulu'iga=in anointing; o le lalolagi;=the earth; ua tumu=filled

samoan_glosses: E pei ona=as; tatou faalogo atu ai,=we (incl) hearken; e faapea ona=saying and; tatou iloa=we (incl) know; i le aai=in the city; a Ieova o 'au,=of the LORD of me; o le aai=the city; a lo tatou Atua;=but our God

samoan_text: O outou le fanau a tagata, tou te faia ma luma lo'u mamalu seia afea? Tou te loto ea i le mea faatauvaa? Tou te saili ea i le pepelo? Selā.
samoan_glosses: O outou=you (all); le fanau=the born; a tagata,=but man; tou te faia=second person plural made; ma luma=and before; lo'u mamalu seia afea? Tou te loto ea=my glory how long?/until when? second person plural heart indeed?/really?; i le mea faatauvaa? Tou te saili ea=in the thing vain second person plural seek indeed?/really?; i le pepelo? Selā.=in the false Selah

samoan_text: Ina fai taulaga ia i taulaga tatau, ma ia outou faalagolago ia Ieova.

samoan_text: E toatele ua fai mai, O ai ea se faailoa mai se mea lelei ia te i matou? Ieova e, ia e faasaga mai ia te i matou le malamalama o ou fofoga.
samoan_glosses: E toatele=many; ua fai mai,=said; O ai ea=who; se faailoa mai=a revealed from; se mea lelei=a thing good; ia te i matou?=unto in we (excl); Ieova e,=O LORD!; ia e faasaga=to to face

samoan_text: O lea e faaumatia ai oe e faavavau e le Atua; na te aveeseina ma liaiina oe ai le fale ie; na te liaiina foi oe ai le nuu o e ola. Selā.

samoan_text: Le Atua e, ia faasilisiliina oe i le lagi; ia i ai i le lalolagi uma lou mamalu.

samoan_text: Ia e faaumatia i latou i le toasa, ia e faaumatia lava, ina ia leai so latou e toe; ia latou iloa o loo pule le Atua ia Iakopo, ia oo lava i tulu'iga o le lalolagi. Selā.
samoan_glosses: Ia e faaumatia i latou=to to destroyed them; i le toasa,=in the wrath; ia e faaumatia lava,=to to destroyed indeed; ina ia leai so latou=so that no so they; e toe;=to again; ia latou iloa=to they know; o loo pule=is (progressive) rule; le Atua ia=God to
kjv_text: Consume them in wrath, consume them, that they may not be: and let them know that God ruleth in Jacob unto the ends of the earth. Selah.
issue: Theological term גָּאוֹן (gaon) = pride, majesty, swelling, thicket not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ua leai sa'u pagota, a ua latou taufetuli mai, ma latou sauniuni. Ia e tulai mai e faafetaiai mai ia te au, ma e silasila mai.
samoan_glosses: Ua leai sa'u=there was not come; pagota,=pagota; a=but; ua latou taufetuli mai,=they running from; ma latou sauniuni.=and they prepared; Ia e tulai=to to arise up; mai=from; e faafetaiai mai ia te au,=to meet from me
kjv_text: They run and prepare themselves without my fault: awake to help me, and behold.
issue: Theological term נֶפֶשׁ (nephesh) = soul, living being, life force not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ua leai sa'u pagota, a ua latou taufetuli mai, ma latou sauniuni. Ia e tulai mai e faafetaiai mai ia te au, ma e silasila mai.
samoan_glosses: Ua leai sa'u=there was not come; pagota,=pagota; a=but; ua latou taufetuli mai,=they running from; ma latou sauniuni.=and they prepared; Ia e tulai=to to arise up; mai=from; e faafetaiai mai ia te au,=to meet from me
kjv_text: They run and prepare themselves without my fault: awake to help me, and behold.
issue: Theological term פֶּשַׁע (pesha) = transgression, rebellion not found in Samoan glosses

issue: Theological term חַטָּאת (chattat) = sin, sin offering not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ua latou foi mai i le afiafi, ua latou fetagisi e pei o le ulī, ma latou faataamilo ane i le aai.
samoan_glosses: Ua latou foi mai=they returned; i le afiafi,=in the evening; ua latou fetagisi=they wept; e pei o le ulī,=like the dog; ma latou faataamilo ane=and they surround; i le aai.=in the city
kjv_text: They return at evening: they make a noise like a dog, and go round about the city.
issue: Theological term עָוֹן (avon) = iniquity, guilt, punishment not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Faauta, ua luai mai upu leaga i o latou gutu; ua i ai pelu i o latou laugutu; auā o ai se faalogo ane?
samoan_glosses: Faauta,=behold; ua luai mai=luai from; upu leaga=word evil; i o latou=in of they; gutu;=mouth; ua=ua; i ai=therein; pelu=sword
kjv_text: Behold, they belch out with their mouth: swords are in their lips: for who, say they, doth hear?
issue: Theological term שׁוּב (shuv) = turn, return, repent not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: E fano ia te oe o e tala pepelo; e matuā inosia e Ieova le tagata ua totolima ma le tau'ole'ole.
samoan_glosses: E fano ia te oe=to perish unto you; o=o; e tala pepelo;=to side false; e matuā inosia=to exceedingly abominable; e Ieova=the LORD; le tagata=the man; ua totolima=totolima; ma le tau'ole'ole.=and the tau'ole'ole
kjv_text: Thou shalt destroy them that speak leasing: the LORD will abhor the bloody and deceitful man.
issue: Theological term עָוֹן (avon) = iniquity, guilt, punishment not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ieova e, ia e ta'ita'i mai ia te au i lau amiotonu, ona o e ita mai ia te au; ia e faasa'o i o'u luma lou ala.
samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; ia e ta'ita'i=to to lead; mai ia te au=from me; i lau amiotonu,=in your righteous; ona o=because of; e ita mai ia te au;=to angry from me; ia e faasa'o i o'u luma=to to spare before me; lou ala.=your way
kjv_text: Lead me, O LORD, in thy righteousness because of mine enemies; make thy way straight before my face.
issue: Theological term חֶסֶד (chesed) = covenant loyalty, steadfast love not found in Samoan glosses

samoan_text: Ieova e, ia e ta'ita'i mai ia te au i lau amiotonu, ona o e ita mai ia te au; ia e faasa'o i o'u luma lou ala.

samoan_glosses: Ieova e,=O LORD!; ia e ta'ita'i=to to lead; mai ia te au=from me; i lau amiotonu,=in your righteous; ona o=because of; e ita mai ia te au;=to angry from me; ia e faasa'o i o'u luma=to to spare before me; lou ala.=your way
